name: 000.0000.001 - Ceiling Tiles
description: Generates a layout of ceiling tiles across an interior workplace floor plate, accounting for room boundaries and MEP clash zones. Outputs tile geometry and a basic quantity schedule.
author: your name
inputs: Room boundary (closed curve from Revit room)
Tile dimensions (W x H in mm)
outputs: Placed ceiling tile family instances in Revit
Tile layout grid as reference lines
Quantity schedule (tile count, cut tile count, total area)
how_to_use: Ensure the ceiling tile family is loaded in the Revit model before running
Select the room or floor boundary to populate
Set tile width and height in the input nodes
Set the grid starting point / offset to control where cut tiles fall
Run the script — tiles will be placed automatically within the boundary
Review the generated schedule to check total tile count and cut tile percentage
notes: Tile layout starts from the grid offset point and works outward, so cut tiles are pushed to room edges rather than the center. Irregular room boundaries (non-rectangular) may produce a higher percentage of cut tiles — review the preview before finalizing. Does not currently account for light fixture cutouts.
